job: Clean-room re-verification (second independent verifier session) of the claimed kill of Sun Conjecture 4.6 (arXiv:2108.07723v7)
verifier: independent verifier agent (Claude Fable 5), session 2026-06-12; all cleanroom2_* artifacts in this directory written from scratch this session -- no code reused from the attack pipeline (driver.py/ryser_mod.c/checker.py/mycheck_2026-06-12.py) nor from the first clean-room layer (independent_checker.py/crt_verify.py/perm_modq.c)

statement_pinned.source: paper.tex (arXiv source): definitions at lines 285-296 (Theorem Th-sin = Thm 1.6, s_n and s'_p PROVEN integers, published values in Remark Rem-sin lines 297-307); conjecture at lines 1109-1114, the 6th conjecture environment of Section 4; numbering '4.6' cross-confirmed in the compiled PDF text (sun-permanents.txt line ~2495)
statement_pinned.conjecture_4_6: (i) odd composite n>1 => s_n == 0 (mod n); (ii) odd prime p: s_p < 0 <=> p == 5 (mod 12), AND s'_p < 0 <=> p == 7 (mod 8)

counterexample_verified.violations: clause 1 demands s_29 < 0 but s_29 > 0; clause 2 demands s'_29 >= 0 (29 != 7 mod 8) but s'_29 < 0 -- both iff clauses of 4.6(ii) fail at p = 29, so Conjecture 4.6 (conjunction of (i) and (ii)) is false as stated

supplementary_also_verified_this_session.note: s_53 and s'_61 had previously rested on the two prior CRT layers only; this session re-derived them from scratch as well, removing that provenance caveat

counterexample_verified.proven_congruence_consistency: s_p == (-1)^((p+1)/2) (mod p) and s'_p == 1 (mod p) (Thm 1.6(iii), PROVEN) hold on all five verified values

method_A_exact_crt.description: Ring homomorphism Z[zeta_4n] -> F_q for fresh random 60-bit primes q == 1 (mod 4n) (own deterministic Miller-Rabin, seed 0x20260612); entries 2sin(2pi jk/n) = -i(z^jk - z^-jk), i = zeta_4n^n; sqrt(n) via quadratic Gauss sum with Gauss's sign theorem (per-prime asserts: i^2 == -1, G_image^2 == n); own Gray-code Ryser permanent mod q (self-tested vs permutation-expansion permanent on 36 random signed matrices); rigorous magnitude bounds (|s_n| <= 2^m m!; |s'_p| <= p^(m+1) m!/4^m via |2sin(2pi t/p)| >= 4/p); symmetric CRT with 2B < prod(q) uniqueness; one HELD-OUT prime per value must agree with the reconstruction. Anchors: all 19 published values of Remark 1.6 reproduced exactly (incl. negatives s_5, s_17, s'_7, s'_23) BEFORE testing any claim.
method_A_exact_crt.results: s_29 = 1053859, s'_29 = -4806838304, s_41 = 5574476521, all exact, all matching the attack's claims

method_B_interval.files: cleanroom2_checker.py (Method B section of same log)
method_B_interval.description: 192-bit mpmath interval arithmetic, direct real sin/csc entries, NO Gauss sums and NO finite fields (closes any global-sign-convention hole in Method A); same Gray-code Ryser over intervals; final enclosure must have width << 1 and contain exactly one integer.
method_B_interval.results: s_5 = -1, s_17 = -239, s'_7 = -6, s'_23 = -7094142 (paper anchors); s_29 = 1053859 (width 5e-44), s'_29 = -4806838304 (width 1e-39), s_41 = 5574476521 (width 5e-37) -- each enclosure contains exactly the claimed integer

method_C_supplement.description: Own OpenMP C Gray-code Ryser mod q (WSL gcc, __uint128 mulmod, modular critical-section fold) driven by the same homomorphism construction with a fresh prime seed (0xC1EA62); C path validated against paper anchors s_23, s'_23 before testing claims; same CRT-uniqueness + held-out-prime discipline.
method_C_supplement.results: s_53 = 1540679755916971 (2 CRT + 1 held-out primes) and s'_61 = -2904784276786469053142518062479 (4 CRT + 1 held-out primes), both matching the attack's claims; re-run after the audit fixes reproduced identical values

codex_adversarial_audit.verdict: KILL STANDS; no normalization or sign escape in the paper; no core false-pass flaw in the verification
codex_adversarial_audit.details: Confirmed statement/numbering from paper.tex + compiled PDF; audited Ryser sign, homomorphism/Gauss-sum equivariance, csc derivation, magnitude bounds, CRT uniqueness, interval layer; recomputed independently with its own direct high-precision real Ryser (no files of mine): s_29 -> 1053859 (err ~2e-132 at 140 dps), s'_29 -> -4806838304 (err ~1.4e-128), s_41 -> 5574476521 (err ~3e-66 at 80 dps); verified all congruences and residue classes.
codex_adversarial_audit.findings_and_remediation: Two non-computational caveats found, both fixed and re-run this session: (1) importing cleanroom2_checker from the supplement truncated cleanroom2_checker.log (log now opened lazily; checker re-run end-to-end with the same seed, identical values); (2) latent uint64 overflow in the C helper's OpenMP '+' reduction of per-thread totals (replaced with a modular critical-section fold; rebuilt; supplement re-run with identical results -- Codex had already checked output invariance across 24/32 threads on the saved heavy csc61 job before the fix)

verdict: KILL CONFIRMED. Conjecture 4.6(ii) of arXiv:2108.07723v7 is false at p = 29 (both iff clauses), with additional failures at p = 41 and 53 (mod-12 clause) and p = 61 (mod-8 clause), all re-derived exactly from scratch this session. Part (i) is NOT refuted and survives as a standalone conjecture.
